mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 6-month average return in periods where HOUST is above the 6-month moving average (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 12-month moving average signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 12-month average return in periods where INDPRO is below the 12-month moving average (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above the 6-month moving average signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 6-month average return in periods where UNRATE is above the 6-month moving average (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income (RSAFS) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 6-month average return in periods where RSAFS is above the 6-month moving average (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 2% signals a monetary easing cycle, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 6-month average return in periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2% (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening labor market, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 6-month average return in periods where PAYEMS is above the 6-month moving average (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures (CPIAUCSL) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 6-month average return in periods where CPIAUCSL is above the 6-month moving average (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above the 6-month moving average and a 2-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above the 6-month moving average signals a strengthening supply chain and labor market, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 6-month average return in periods where INDPRO and PAYEMS are both above the 6-month moving average (t-test p < 0.05).
